senses.definition: the upper front part of the human chest that contains the breasts in women and the pectoral muscles in men
examples: She covered her breasts with a scarf.
Breast cancer screening is important for early detection.

senses.definition: the front part of a garment that covers the chest area; a bra or chest covering
examples: She wore a pink cardigan over her breast.
The jacket has a quilted breast section.

senses.definition: the front part of a poultry or animal body, especially the breast meat
examples: We grilled the chicken breasts.
Turkey breast is lean protein.

senses.definition: a band worn around the chest, historically used as a supportive or decorative item (rare; specialized term)
examples: The ancient costume included a decorative breastband.
In some ceremonial outfits, a breastband indicates rank.

senses.definition: a beam or structural member that runs across the breast of a building or object (rare architectural term)
examples: The breastbeam supports the upper frame of the gateway.
In timber framing, the breastbeam ties the posts together.

senses.definition: the sternum; a flat bone at the center of the chest to which ribs are attached
examples: The doctor felt his breastbone for any fracture.
Chest compressions are performed on the breastbone during CPR.

senses.definition: plural of breastbone; the sternums
examples: The ancient skeletons showed broken breastbones.
Two breastbones were found in the chest cavity.

senses.definition: having a breast or breasts; used in some animal descriptions
examples: A bird with a bright crested breaststed plumage.
The garment was breasted with decorative fabric.

senses.definition: an obsolete or dialect term for a person who breastholds or a breastpiece; not common in modern usage
examples: In the old text, the word breaster appears as a craftsman.
The manuscript mentions a breaster as part of armor.

senses.definition: an archaic or dialect term for a meal taken at the breast of a person or during nursing; not common in modern English
examples: The old diary mentions a breastfast at dawn.
Breastfast is not used in contemporary cooking terms.

senses.definition: to feed a baby with milk from a mother's breast
examples: Many babies are breastfed for the first six months.
The mother breastfed the infant during the night.

senses.definition: to feed a baby with milk from the mother's breast
examples: She will breastfeed on demand.
Breastfeed your baby in a quiet room.

senses.definition: the act or practice of feeding a baby with milk from the mother's breast
examples: Breastfeeding provides important nutrients for babies.
Support for breastfeeding caregivers is common in hospitals.

senses.definition: plural of breastfeeding; instances or occurrences of breastfeeding
examples: The study tracked several breastfeedings per day.
Policies support multiple breastfeedings in childcare settings.

senses.definition: third-person singular present tense of breastfeed
examples: She breastfeeds her baby during the night.
Many mothers breastfeeds for several months.

senses.definition: full to the chest or containing much breast area; not a common standard word
examples: The garment appeared breastful to cover modestly.
A breastful of fabric hung loose.

senses.definition: an anatomical term for the distance or height at the chest; extremely uncommon
examples: The chart lists breastheight as a measure.

senses.definition: a tool or hook used around the breast area of a garment or in metalwork; historically a term in armor or harness
examples: The leatherworker used a breasthook to shape the buckle.
A breasthook helps secure the breastplate.

senses.definition: an informal or affectionate term for a breast or close friend; dialect usage
examples: He called his sister 'breastie' as a term of endearment.
A 'breastie' can refer to a cherished companion in some dialects.

senses.definition: to move or pass across the chest area; or to rise to face a problem
examples: The swimmer was breasting the waves.
She is breasting the challenge with courage.

senses.definition: without a breast or without prominent chest features; rarely used
examples: The costume was breastless and open at the front.

senses.definition: a mark or scar on the breast; a term found in medical or anatomical descriptions
examples: The breastmark indicated a previous surgery.
Physicians note any breastmark during examination.

senses.definition: a piece of armor or clothing protecting the chest; a breastplate
examples: The knight wore a polished breastpiece as part of his armor.
The breastpiece protected vital organs during battle.

senses.definition: a decorative pin worn on the chest, over the breast area
examples: She pinned a breastpin to her blouse.
The nurse wore a simple breastpin for identification.

senses.definition: plural of breastpin; decorative pins worn on the chest
examples: A row of breastpins glinted in the light.
Military uniforms often include breastpins.

senses.definition: a piece of armor that covers the chest and back to protect the torso
examples: The knight tightened his breastplate before the duel.
The armor included a heavy breastplate.

senses.definition: plural of breastplate; multiple chest armor pieces
examples: The museum displayed several ancient breastplates.
Medieval knights wore breastplates as part of their armor.
